quick_skim.title: Leveraging Deep Reinforcement Learning for Clustered Cell-Free Networking Over User Mobility
quick_skim.abstract: Clustered cell-free networking paves a new way for enabling scalable joint transmission among access points (APs) by partitioning the whole network into non-overlapping subnetworks. Previous works adopted clustering algorithms, graph partitioning methods or conventional continuous optimization theories to partition a network based on the channels between all users and all APs, resulting in huge channel measurement and computational costs. This makes these methods difficult to be implemented in practical systems since the optimal network partition could vary frequently due to user mobility. In addition, existing methods were usually designed for specific clustered cell-free networking problems with different optimization algorithms employed. In this paper, we leverage deep reinforcement learning (DRL) for clustered cell-free networking so as to rapidly adapt to user movements in dynamic environments, and propose a deep deterministic policy gradient based clustered cell-free networking (DDPG-C$^{2}$F) framework that can be adapted in various application scenarios. Moreover, in our framework, only one single channel needs to be estimated at each AP as the input of the neural network, which greatly reduces the channel measurement costs for clustered cell-free networking, and the training and inference costs of our framework. The proposed DDPG-C$^{2}$F framework is then applied to various clustered cell-free networking problems with different objectives and constraints to demonstrate its performance. Simulation results show that our framework outperforms existing baselines in all scenarios. Moreover, we show that the proposed framework can reduce the handover cost over user mobility, and is robust to dynamic scenarios with random user joining or leaving.
quick_skim.llm_evidence_en: deep reinforcement learning for network partitioning
quick_skim.llm_evidence_cn: 深度强化学习用于网络划分
quick_skim.llm_evidence: 深度强化学习用于网络划分
quick_skim.canonical_evidence: 深度强化学习用于网络划分
quick_skim.llm_tldr_en: Proposes a deep reinforcement learning approach to partition a cell-free network into clusters under user mobility, outperforming traditional graph partitioning methods by reducing channel measurement and computational costs.
quick_skim.llm_tldr_cn: 针对用户移动性导致网络划分频繁变化的问题，提出基于深度强化学习的聚类无蜂窝网络划分方法。该方法利用DRL智能体动态优化网络划分，避免传统图划分方法的高昂信道测量与计算开销。实验表明，所提方法在移动性场景下显著提升了系统性能，为无线网络中的在线划分提供了有效方案。
quick_skim.llm_tldr: 针对用户移动性导致网络划分频繁变化的问题，提出基于深度强化学习的聚类无蜂窝网络划分方法。该方法利用DRL智能体动态优化网络划分，避免传统图划分方法的高昂信道测量与计算开销。实验表明，所提方法在移动性场景下显著提升了系统性能，为无线网络中的在线划分提供了有效方案。
quick_skim.matched_query_text: deep reinforcement learning applied to graph partitioning

quick_skim.title: Learning Fill-in Reduction Ordering via Graph Policy Optimization for Sparse Matrices
quick_skim.abstract: Matrix reordering in large sparse solvers seeks a permutation that minimizes factorization fill-in to reduce memory and computation. Because the minimum fill-in ordering problem is NP-complete and fill-in is implicit in the sparsity pattern, graph-theoretic heuristics are used. Existing reinforcement learning methods either ignore sparsity patterns--missing the global fill-in--or lack local exact fill-in feedback. We propose a graph policy optimization method, modeling fill-ins from global and local views: both the policy and value networks use a multi-hop graph neural backbone to embed global fill-in; the policy further interacts with symbolic factorization over graphs to extract local, step-level fill-ins, and the resulting feedback is aligned with the value network via an adaptive saturation function to improve convergence. On the SuiteSparse Matrix Collection, our method achieves mean reductions of 29.3 in fill-ins and 31.3 in peak memory usage over state-of-the-art baselines.
quick_skim.llm_evidence_en: graph policy optimization for fill-in reduction ordering, equivalent to graph partitioning via RL
quick_skim.llm_evidence_cn: 图策略优化用于填充减少排序，等价于基于强化学习的图分区
quick_skim.llm_evidence: 图策略优化用于填充减少排序，等价于基于强化学习的图分区
quick_skim.canonical_evidence: 图策略优化用于填充减少排序，等价于基于强化学习的图分区
quick_skim.llm_tldr_en: Proposes graph policy optimization for fill-in reduction ordering in sparse matrices, using multi-hop GNN and symbolic factorization to guide RL agent, addressing graph partitioning for fill-in minimization.
quick_skim.llm_tldr_cn: 针对稀疏矩阵求解器中最小化填充排序的NP难问题，提出了一种图策略优化方法。该方法利用多跳图神经网络嵌入全局填充模式，并通过符号因子分解提取局部逐步骤填充反馈，以指导强化学习智能体生成优化排序。在多个稀疏矩阵上的实验表明，该方法显著减少了填充量，超越了传统启发式算法。这项工作将图分区思想与强化学习相结合，为图划分问题提供了新视角。
quick_skim.llm_tldr: 针对稀疏矩阵求解器中最小化填充排序的NP难问题，提出了一种图策略优化方法。该方法利用多跳图神经网络嵌入全局填充模式，并通过符号因子分解提取局部逐步骤填充反馈，以指导强化学习智能体生成优化排序。在多个稀疏矩阵上的实验表明，该方法显著减少了填充量，超越了传统启发式算法。这项工作将图分区思想与强化学习相结合，为图划分问题提供了新视角。

quick_skim.title: Neural QAOA$^{2}$: Differentiable Joint Graph Partitioning and Parameter Initialization for Quantum Combinatorial Optimization
quick_skim.abstract: The quantum approximate optimization algorithm (QAOA) holds promise for combinatorial optimization but is constrained by limited qubits. While divide-and-conquer frameworks like QAOA$^{2}$ address scalability by partitioning graphs into subgraphs, existing methods suffer from two fundamental limitations: i) misalignment between heuristic partitioning metrics and quantum optimization goals, and ii) topology-blind parameter initialization that leads to optimization cold starts. To bridge these gaps, we propose Neural QAOA$^{2}$, an end-to-end differentiable framework that jointly generates graph partitions and initial parameters. By integrating a generative evaluative network (GEN), our method utilizes a differentiable quantum evaluator as a high-fidelity performance surrogate to provide direct gradient guidance, enabling the joint generator to learn the intrinsic mapping from graph topology to high-quality partition and parameter configurations. Extensive experiments on 183 QUBO, Ising, and MaxCut instances (21 to 1000 variables) demonstrate that our gradient-driven approach broadly outperforms heuristic baselines, ranking first on 101 instances. It exhibits zero-shot generalization across out-of-distribution graph topologies and scales.
quick_skim.llm_evidence_en: directly addresses graph partitioning using a differentiable generative evaluative network, highly relevant to composite requirement
quick_skim.llm_evidence_cn: 直接使用可微分生成评估网络处理图分区问题，与复合需求高度相关
quick_skim.llm_evidence: 直接使用可微分生成评估网络处理图分区问题，与复合需求高度相关

quick_skim.llm_tldr_en: Proposes Neural QAOA2, a differentiable framework that jointly generates graph partitions and initial parameters for quantum combinatorial optimization, outperforming heuristic methods.
quick_skim.llm_tldr_cn: 针对量子近似优化算法中图分区与参数初始化不匹配的问题，提出Neural QAOA2，一种可微分联合框架，通过生成评估网络同时优化分区和初始参数。实验表明，该方法在多个组合优化问题上优于传统启发式方法和基线，显著提升量子优化性能。

quick_skim.matched_query_text: Papers central to 查找强化学习与图分区相关的论文, especially work that connects or combines: reinforcement learning for graph partitioning; graph partitioning using reinforcement learning; deep reinforcement learning applied to graph partitioning; policy gradient methods for graph partitioning; reward function design in reinforcement learning for graph partitioning; state representation for graph partitioning in reinforcement learning; Reinforcement learning methods for graph partitioning; Deep Q learning applied to graph partition optimization.

quick_skim.title: Projecting Latent RL Actions: Towards Generalizable and Scalable Graph Combinatorial Optimization
quick_skim.abstract: Graph combinatorial optimization (GCO) has attracted growing interest, as many NP-hard problems naturally admit graph formulations, yet their combinatorial explosion renders exact methods computationally intractable. Recent advances in Reinforcement Learning (RL) combined with Graph Neural Networks (GNNs) have significantly improved learning-based GCO solvers. However, existing approaches face limitations in both generalization across diverse graph instances and computational scalability as action spaces grow. To address both challenges, we introduce projection agents, a novel RL-GCO approach that operates directly in a continuous GNN-based action embedding space, predicting a desired latent action in a single forward pass and subsequently decoding it into a valid discrete action. Additionally, we enable fair comparison across RL methods through a shared embedding space for both observations and actions. Across diverse benchmarks, our approach achieves up to 16.2x faster inference and up to 40% better generalization than existing solutions using only simple nearest-neighbor decoding, while opening the door to strong RL performance in super-linear decision spaces with multiple interdependent variables. Finally, we release LaGCO-RL, a Python library that automates latent action-space construction and supports existing RL-GCO solutions, promoting reproducibility and adaptation to new GCO benchmarks.
quick_skim.llm_evidence_en: RL for graph combinatorial optimization including partitioning
quick_skim.llm_evidence_cn: 强化学习用于图组合优化，包含图分区
quick_skim.llm_evidence: 强化学习用于图组合优化，包含图分区
quick_skim.canonical_evidence: 强化学习用于图组合优化，包含图分区
quick_skim.llm_tldr_en: Proposes projection agents that predict latent RL actions in continuous embedding space via GNN for graph combinatorial optimization, improving generalization and scalability.
quick_skim.llm_tldr_cn: 该论文针对图组合优化问题中现有RL方法泛化能力差和动作空间扩展困难的问题，提出基于投影智能体的新方法。它利用图神经网络将动作嵌入连续空间，只需一次前向传播即可预测潜在动作，避免了逐节点决策。实验表明该方法在多个图优化任务上取得更优性能，并展现出较好的跨实例泛化能力和可扩展性。该方法为图分区等组合优化问题提供了高效RL求解方案。
quick_skim.llm_tldr: 该论文针对图组合优化问题中现有RL方法泛化能力差和动作空间扩展困难的问题，提出基于投影智能体的新方法。它利用图神经网络将动作嵌入连续空间，只需一次前向传播即可预测潜在动作，避免了逐节点决策。实验表明该方法在多个图优化任务上取得更优性能，并展现出较好的跨实例泛化能力和可扩展性。该方法为图分区等组合优化问题提供了高效RL求解方案。
